articles.title: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro
articles.summary: A 32B parameter flagship LLM designed for enterprise-grade complexity through reasoning. Addresses challenges in Korean-language and domain-specific scenarios where scaling alone is insufficient.

articles.title: OpenAI's Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it's shutting down
articles.summary: OpenAI is shutting down the Sora video generator after 15 months. Despite impressive video and audio generation capabilities, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

articles.title: OpenAI's plans to make ChatGPT more like Amazon aren't going so well
articles.summary: OpenAI is moving away from Instant Checkout, which allowed users to buy items directly through the ChatGPT interface. The feature failed to gain traction in making ChatGPT more commerce-oriented.

articles.title: Anthropic's Claude Code gets 'safer' auto mode
articles.summary: Anthropic launched an auto mode for Claude Code that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The feature offers a safer alternative between constant handholding and giving dangerous levels of autonomy.

articles.title: Building AI-powered GitHub issue triage with the Copilot SDK
articles.summary: Learn how to integrate the Copilot SDK into a React Native app to generate AI-powered issue summaries. Covers production patterns for graceful degradation and caching.

articles.title: Malicious litellm_init.pth in litellm 1.82.8 — credential stealer
articles.summary: Security alert about malicious code found in litellm package version 1.82.8 that steals credentials. Critical security issue affecting the popular LLM integration library.

articles.title: Auto mode for Claude Code
articles.summary: Discussion about Anthropic's new auto mode feature for Claude Code that enables more autonomous AI operation. Covers the balance between automation and safety controls.

articles.title: When AI Shows Its Work, Is It Actually Working? Step-Level Evaluation Reveals Frontier Language Models Frequently Bypass Their Own Reasoning
articles.summary: Research reveals that language models often generate decorative reasoning steps after already deciding on answers, rather than genuinely using their step-by-step reasoning. Step-level evaluation shows models frequently bypass their own stated reasoning process.

articles.title: LLM Olympiad: Why Model Evaluation Needs a Sealed Exam
articles.summary: Argues that current LLM benchmarks are easily manipulated by benchmark-chasing and test contamination. Proposes an Olympiad-style sealed exam approach to provide more reliable evaluation of model capabilities.

articles.title: Lie to Me: How Faithful Is Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Reasoning Models?
articles.summary: Extends evaluation of chain-of-thought faithfulness beyond proprietary models, finding acknowledgment rates as low as 25% for Claude and 39% for DeepSeek-R1. Questions the reliability of CoT reasoning as a transparency mechanism.

articles.title: Whether, Not Which: Mechanistic Interpretability Reveals Dissociable Affect Reception and Emotion Categorization in LLMs
articles.summary: First study to test whether LLM emotion circuits detect genuine emotional meaning or just emotion keywords. Uses mechanistic interpretability to reveal separate systems for affect reception and emotion categorization.

articles.title: Set-Valued Prediction for Large Language Models with Feasibility-Aware Coverage Guarantees
articles.summary: Proposes moving from single point predictions to set-valued predictions in LLMs to better capture model uncertainty. Provides formal coverage guarantees while acknowledging that valid answers may exist beyond the top-ranked response.

articles.title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
articles.summary: Kleiner Perkins raises $3.5B for AI investments, including $1B for early-stage startups and $2.5B for late-stage growth businesses. Signals major VC commitment to AI sector growth.

articles.title: Disney cancels $1 billion OpenAI partnership amid Sora shutdown plans
articles.summary: Disney's $1 billion OpenAI partnership ends as Sora shuts down, with reports suggesting Disney was blindsided by the decision. No money reportedly changed hands in the failed partnership.

articles.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
articles.summary: A major AI company reportedly offered $26 million to build a data center on a Kentucky family's farm, which was rejected. Highlights the massive infrastructure needs and land acquisition efforts for AI development.

articles.title: Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
articles.summary: Arm is producing its first CPU after 35 years, developed in collaboration with Meta as the first customer. Represents a significant strategic shift for the chip design company.

articles.title: Package Managers Need to Cool Down
articles.summary: Commentary on issues with package management systems in the AI/ML ecosystem. Discusses the need for better stability and security practices in dependency management.

articles.title: Quoting Christopher Mims
articles.summary: Simon Willison shares insights from Christopher Mims on current AI developments. Provides community perspective on recent AI trends and developments.
